attempt: Move from bounded stateful certificates to global compactness pressure: finite decreasing quantities matter only if they can be upgraded into a theorem that rules out all infinite obstruction paths.
obstruction: A finite cutoff ledger still needs a theorem that no error term reopens beyond the cutoff.
candidate_theorem: A global cutoff compactness theorem proves every ledger state beyond N0 has positive representation count.
next_experiment: Audit whether major/minor arc error states form a monotone cone under increasing n.
claim_boundary: No goldbach proof and no certified goldbach counterexample.
